chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P.No.D-2552 of 2019 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on MA-11620/19 2. For orders on office objection 3. For orders on MA-11621/19 4. For orders on MA-11622/19 5. For hearing of main case. 21.11.2019. Mr. Ghulam Haider Chandio, advocate for petitioner. = 1. Urgency granted. 2. Overruled. 3. Exemption granted. 4&5 As per petitioner she being Lady Health Worker is working at LMC eye hospital at Hyderabad and in possession of Quarter No.D/8 LMC Eye Hospital, Hyderabad, therefrom she is going to be dispossessed by the respondents. By submitting so, she sought for direction against the respondents to allot the said quarter to her and refrain from dispossessing her from the subject quarter. It is contended by learned counsel for the petitioner that the petitioner has taken birth in the subject quarter, which previously was allotted to her relatives; the petitioner is entitled to its allotment and her dispossession therefrom would be illegal being low paid employee. We have considered the above arguments and perused the record. If, for the sake of arguments; it is believed that the subject quarter is in possession of the petitioner, then such possession with her is illegal and her status over it is that of trespasser. No trespasser could not be permitted to retain the unauthorized possession under any circumstances. Simultaneously, the entitlement of the allotment of the quarter relating to the terms of 2 the service could not be resolved by this Court in exercise of its constitutional jurisdiction under the pretext that the petitioner is low paid employee.

No trespasser could not be permitted to retain the unauthorized possession under any circumstances. Simultaneously, the entitlement of the allotment of the quarter relating to the terms of 2 the service could not be resolved by this Court in exercise of its constitutional jurisdiction under the pretext that the petitioner is low paid employee. The Instant constitutional petition being misconceived is dismissed in limini along with the listed applications. JUDGE JUDGE Ahmed/Pa,
